Copy blue print - week 2 – Session 3
The power of near instant persuasion and the booty of captive ecstatic clients
This is violently important (word alchemy)
jb@jonbenson.com
Access to copypro starts this week
Hotseats can be on the hotseat only if the homework have been posted to the Facebook group
The first 2 get more hot seat action
Notes from hot seats:
Derrick:
Dumb down content – do not assume people know the words used
Copy is simplicity (KISS)
Use short sentences consisting of a few words
Remove speed bumps – strip to the bare minimum
2 - 3 syllable words are the best 4 syllables is pushing it for the 4th grade reading level
“Specifically” – can be used to get people to stop and pay attention
John Carlton – starts with bullets. These are the most concise way to think things. Use this
approach to write copy
The short sentences gets people to look
Record the copy vocally to see how it sounds … short sentences sound better (read it like a VSL
… in rhythm) – Use vocal pitch and tone (underlined words as well as italicized)
NLP commands like “listen” and “look” cause people to read the copy
“Right now” “in real time” are a present language patterns in copy
Real time – next time is a way to connect the copy
Don’t worry about grammar
Oscar :
Its something I like to call – way to get people to pay attention by branding the idea
For you – heavy lifting, all the work, all the bragging (use for you as a way to show the reader
how much work is done for them)
Idea
Idea
Idea
Without (pain)
What can be done instead? (future pacing)
Short answers in present tense
“Use take your dream vacation” – instead of go to bora bora (what if people hate tropical places
– they are weird) get them to imagine – picture words can be used
Use the gist of the subject to make a short sentence
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

How short can I say the same thing – get used to trimming all sentences down to the bare
minimum but still be impactful
Make people think though ideas in a story (Amazon example)
Avatars: Authors – tier 2 from Jon – assume they are there already (how do they view
themselves – what group do they aspire to be in that would be a complement or an upgrade)
Let me be real (assumes you were not real before) .. use “now you know this is true”
Session 3 – Training
Brand everything I can, I will own them. People (my avatar will start using the names or
monikers I give them) – Jon Benson advice
The power of self identifying avatars.
Avatar – the person or group I am writing to
Self identifying Avatar (SIA) – Are the labels the prospect identifies with or wishes they had.
This creates fans by creating a movement – the part of their brain says - I know that I can do that.
People will live up to their name in their mind. This is a form of action for the reader even if they
don’t take any.
By creating a group they are already on the inside – They want to be included and with a name
they are.
Names matter – If you call someone something they have the ability to identify to that level (self
identity) Great self identifying avatars (SIA) are short 1- 3 words … 1 word is best when
possible.
Descriptive – something they identify with and a way they would to use to describe themselves –
Author – fat burner
Moniker – A word they would love to use for themselves (I love the way that sounds) this can
build a fan club “I like the way that makes me sound”
Wile E Coyote – Super genius
3 tiers avatars name labels for a reader
Tier 1 – Generic name / this sucks and is what a rookie uses (this sucks)
People confuse avatars with demographics These examples are too broad and not like a secret
society
· Menopausal women over 50
· People who want to lose weight
· People who spent 1k per day on fb ads
· Frequent air traveler
Tier 2 – damn good
They have the level already but not kick ass society
Examples
Menopausal women over 50
· Menopausal survivor
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

Men over 35 who want to lose weight – You don’t always have to remind them of their avatar
name when its generic
· Dieters

avatars)
People who spend 1k per day
· Marketers
· Ad managers
Frequent air traveler
· Flyer
· Frequent flier
Tier 3 epic avatar
Branded by myself – this creates a posse – The more I brand the more people identify with me.
Create a brand for the letter I write. A Self Identifying Avatar (SIA) brands the people I speak to
This is the overlay recipe in a nut shell for Jon Benson (duality) the more branded the more
people associate with me and only me (when possible) Think “How do I think of myself” for the
Self Identifying Avatar (SIA). The group has to want to identify with the name… it cant be
stupid
1. 	Empathy
2. 	Persuasion
Menopausal women over 50
· Silver sages
· Menowarriors (aggressive)
· Menogoddesses (soft)
Men over 35 who want to lose weight
· Fatburners (simple)
· Sixpackers (big desire)
· Waistline warriors (appeals to combat)
People who spend 1k per day
· Facebooker (cute)
· Adders
· ROIers (presupposition)
Frequent air travelers
· World travelers
· Global citizens
All of these are tier 3 and makes a group – a group can be moved and herded. People want to be
part of an inclusive movement. People like to identify with a label
Tier 3 Examples:

-- 3 of 6 --

· Digital marketers
· Coffee drinkers (pro musicians bass player posse)
· 10%ers (personal development)
· Emailers
· Lovelighters (spiritual dating site)
· Iron warriors (fitness)
· Supers (internet marketing super friends)
· Copypro (copywriter)
· Funnel hackers (Rus Brun)
Most people will want to join as a club – this is how to look at an avatar Give them a name and
make them feel special
· Tier 1 – This sucks
· Tier 2 – Get here at the minimum
· Tier 3 – Now there is a group / extra credit (1 to 3 words)
Problems vs complaints
People have problems that they complain about – Every avatar has complaints and a problem
The complaint can be a want in reverse – A wish of a better life but the prospect doesn’t know
how to get there without help – Its easier to complain than ask for help in some instances. People
look for “done for you” guides, classes and even assistance personally. When they find a group
that relates it makes their problem real and now it can be fixed. Now there are other people like
them. Identity is a huge way to persuade people to change, purchase or take action.
Some people are really trying to build rapport by complaining and looking for people with
similar traits.
This can be a bitchfest that is superficial but important (whaaa, uh, I wish I could find a date)
The real problem isn’t on their mind… it’s the complaint. They are explaining the symptoms and
not the actually problem.
1. 	They may not know the problem or how to fix it
2. 	They want someone or something with the “magic” done for you solution
Problem: Blood factor X (this is fake and for the class BFX isn’t real) The problem is now
branded and has a name for the reader. This makes it real and now has the ability to have a
solution.
Symptoms: Getting hot all of the time – being easily winded – being unable to lose weight
Blood factor X is a brand – a branded problem can be “fixed” because it has been identified
Bring in the problem with the fear and have the only solution for the reader
Write to 1 problem and one problem only no matter how many complaints – they can have tons
of complaints.
Put all of the complaints in a nice package and offer a solution under one name for all of them.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 6 --

Perry Belcher – The idea is to never ask the reader to believe more than one things at a time
(relate to their complaints)
Take all of the complaints and then name them. Reframe the problem and give a solution. The
solution now can be found because the problem has a name. The person can be a part or a group
of people who have this problem.
Copypro uses complaints rather than top of mind impact
Primary cause is used in copypro (the label for the problem)
1 problem = 1 solution – easier for the reader
Primary complaints – the #1 complaint when they think about it
Secondary complaint – all of the things they may or may not know go along with their primary
complaint
Copypro uses – (verb) (pronoun) (complaint) as a basic formula. This keeps data consistent.
Pronouns are optional
Examples
· losing money on Facebook
· Packing on belly fat
· Hiding your desire for connection
Verb first is a better complaint because it can be visceral and have resonation. The better the verb
the better the interaction.
The same (verb) (pronoun) (primary complaint) works for the secondary complaints. They
should always relate back to the primary
Examples - the primary complaint leads into the secondary complaints (its almost like
mind reading)
· Losing money on Facebook – Adding stress to your marriage, spending a fortune on “guru”
courses
· Packing on the bell fat: Looking at your clothes that no longer fit, getting concerned looks
from friends, comfort eating in hiding
· Hiding your desire for connection: letting yourself go, entraining thoughts of cheating, gazing
at other men or women
All tied together:
When you are packing on belly fat everything feels uncomfortable, perhaps getting concerned
looks from fiends. Maybe even comfort eating at night…
Bullets:
· You are getting concerned looks from friends…
· Your even “comfort eating at night, often alone and hiding…
Teasers:
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 5 of 6 --

· Getting concerned looks from friends? Comfort eating at night?
· Then your packing on the belly fat… and its getting worse.
· Copypro does this work inside the program
Where to find complaints in the real world
Discussions button for google search – plugin on chrome “I cant lose weight” “I cant get a date”
“I cant write good copy” – this is what people say in their own words. This isn’t what I am
thinking
The phrases can be used for the copy since they will be native for the person complaining. As a
side benefit the words can be cut and pasted into a document. A word cloud can be used to sort
through the most used version. The phrases may sound like what the avatar would say to
themselves when they think about the problem.
Q and A
1. 	Word alchemy – thesaurus the words used to give more impact (pick different words)
2. 	Research in the wrong way – demographic and not the avatar
3. 	What is Tier 1 again – boring research names for a product
4. 	Make my ideas the place everyone has to go for the truth
5. 	Can multiple steps be related to 1 step (make it a group) 1 blueprint contains 7things. 1
idea is easier to wrap your head around than a bunch. Tie all of the problems together. “This
blueprint covers the 7 biggest issues you face as a ________”
6. 	How to use happy words for Facebook from session 1 – A Facebook insider gave these
words to Jon. Facebook algorithms look for these words and promote them. These words can be
used in ads or posts.
7. 	Tier 1 – is demographics – tier 1 sucks because it is so generic – it’s a necessary graphic
because this is the starting point – you must get to tier 3 to build a tribe (funnel hacker is a term
for a tribe people use)
8. 	Copypro – do the back end work to make the copy easier – most marketers don’t want to
touch the niche – its too much “work” for them
9. 	Post all homework in Facebook – not in copypro
10. Distinctions between – tiers can be names for ideas - acronyms can be used – the more that
can be branded the better. Every week something will need to be branded.
Not in the course and a reminder for me:
Exercise:
What are the top complaints the buyer has, what do they say about for what they are going
through?
How does the reader describe their problem?
What words can I use and then thesaurus the hell out of to describe it better
